title: Figure 5 from: Sparks V, Kang I, Carrere M, Broadley H, Diaz R (2025) Differential oviposition timing by seasonality and oviposition timing of parasitoids of the invasive roseau cane scale (Nipponaclerda biwakoensis) as revealed by multiplex PCR assays. Journal of Hymenoptera Research 98: 1133-1152. https://doi.org/10.3897/jhr.98.150721
author: Sparks, Violet
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 5 Wasp DNA detection results from RCS using multiplex PCR and comparison with singleplex PCR results. Multiplex PCR results are consistent with singleplex PCR results indicating all results are true positives: 1 1st instar nymphs (n = 20) 2 2nd instar nymphs (n = 10) 3 2nd instar nymphs (n = 5) 4 3rd instar nymphs (n = 5) 5 3rd instar nymphs (n = 3) 6 3rd instar nymph (n = 1) 7 4th instar nymphs (n = 3) 8 4th instar nymphs (n = 2) 9 4th instar nymph (n = 1) 10 adult female scales (n = 3) 11 adult female scales (n = 2) 12 adult female scale (n = 1) 13 male scales (n = 3). The 155 bp band represents B. depressa, the 221 bp band denotes N. japonicus, the 303 bp band indicates Aprostocetus sp., and the 495 bp band represents A. lasallei.
steps_raw: Figure 5 Wasp DNA detection results from RCS using multiplex PCR and comparison with singleplex PCR results. Multiplex PCR results are consistent with singleplex PCR results indicating all results are true positives: 1 1st instar nymphs (n = 20) 2 2nd instar nymphs (n = 10) 3 2nd instar nymphs (n = 5) 4 3rd instar nymphs (n = 5) 5 3rd instar nymphs (n = 3) 6 3rd instar nymph (n = 1) 7 4th instar nymphs (n = 3) 8 4th instar nymphs (n = 2) 9 4th instar nymph (n = 1) 10 adult female scales (n = 3) 11 adult female scales (n = 2) 12 adult female scale (n = 1) 13 male scales (n = 3). The 155 bp band represents B. depressa, the 221 bp band denotes N. japonicus, the 303 bp band indicates Aprostocetus sp., and the 495 bp band represents A. lasallei.
